semantic.role: Pipeline task stage that reconciles the file system state against the indexed cache by detecting new, changed, or removed files, updating file statuses, and clearing stale symbol exports.
responsibilities: Load the language snapshot from the manifest to determine which file extensions are tracked per language.
Query the scanner for all tracked files and filter by the set of tracked extensions.
For each tracked file, calculate a content hash and compare it with the manifest's cached state to decide if an update is needed.
Read index documents, validate their compatibility via `isCompatibleIndexDocument`, and produce SpineUnit records.
Update file status with associated documentation records for new or changed files.
Clear symbol exports for files that no longer exist on disk.
Report index read issues once per incompatible document.
outOfScope: CLI command parsing and argument handling.
Orchestration of unrelated services (e.g., LLM, MCP, view rendering).
File system scanning of untracked extensions (delegated to scanner).
Deep AST extraction beyond index document reading.
invariants: Must only depend on core task contracts (`SpineTask`, `TaskContext`) and infrastructure utilities (manifest, scanner, index-reader).
Must not perform CLI command parsing or service orchestration (rule `task-stage-boundary`).
Must call `reportIndexReadIssueOnce` at most once per incompatible index document to avoid spam.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: ArchSpine v1.0.0 establishes a layered pipeline architecture where each task stage performs a focused, local transformation on project state. ReconcileTask is the stage responsible for maintaining cache consistency between the live file system and the index database.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: 0c6bd73 — ArchSpine v1.0.0 — Architectural Governance & Semantic Layer for the AI Era. Introduces the reconciled pipeline stage as part of the new governance layer.

publicSurface.description: Pipeline task class that synchronizes the file system state with the indexed cache. Invoked by the pipeline runner after scanning and before downstream stages like summarization or validation.
